118894.explanation: Giving an opinion is of course always allowed, but the final decision lies with the municipal council. How and by whom the importance of a topic is determined?

118895.explanation: "Residents" sounds rather broad and non-committal. There are various pitfalls involved.

118897.explanation: The number of farmers within municipal boundaries is low. It is nice to preserve them.

118898.explanation: This possibility already exists in Maassluis.

118899.explanation: Distribution centers are already located in Maassluis and we must try to keep them. Available space has decreased considerably over the past years, so that is fine.

118900.explanation: Asylum reception in Maassluis has been in place for several years. It was previously extended and even expanded from 1 to 2 boats. This seems more than sufficient and whether or not to extend this is a matter for a referendum.

118903.explanation: Of course, consideration for others is important. The municipality may continue to pay attention to this. But banning is too far for us.

118908.explanation: Entrepreneurs have their own responsibility here.

118910.explanation: There are already funds for this, but it could be emphasized a bit more.

118911.explanation: The local broadcaster is WOS, located in Westland. Support is available. Extra money-well, what is extra?

118913.explanation: Opportunities exist. This requires further explanation.

118918.explanation: We find it important that everyone feels safe, and this can certainly contribute to that.

118919.explanation: If this is not yet in place: recently, the topics of femicide and domestic violence have been addressed several times on the political agenda.

118927.explanation: Which threshold and what kind of help? Requires some explanation and nuance.

118929.explanation: There must be a good balance between charging electrically and not charging electrically.

118930.explanation: With the increasing number of road users, every effective measure is welcome.

118931.explanation: Primarily about help and deployment of available capacity and not about anything else.

118936.explanation: Honestly, I wouldn't know where and how.

118938.explanation: Already on the list of housing seekers. Legislation exists about this.

118941.explanation: School swimming was abolished once, but there is money available for swimming lessons for people with a low income.

118945.explanation: There are strict rules for this. Proper inspection is important when issuing permits. The permit duration should also be reduced.

118946.explanation: A lot is already being done about this in Maassluis.

118948.explanation: Livability also means losing access to a car-but within reasonable distance.

118950.explanation: Maassluis already has done and continues to do a lot-much more than the regional municipalities.

118894.explanation: It is important that residents are involved in the neighborhood and decisions in the city. A referendum has less nuance and can increase divisions; therefore, we prefer personal contact through participation and creating support.

118895.explanation: It is important that residents are involved in the neighborhood. Maintaining parks, playgrounds, or container gardens can be taken over by residents. The municipality facilitates residents so they can carry out the work.

118897.explanation: We find it important that all entrepreneurs are supported to run a healthy business. However, this should always be done through the formal channel of permit applications. We find it important that the municipality supports the application process.

118898.explanation: CDA Maassluis considers sustainability important. We see the municipality's role as a driver by sharing good examples. We want to continue the sustainability loan and support residents in other ways.

118899.explanation: CDA Maassluis believes that entrepreneurs with plans in Maassluis should always be engaged in conversation. Given the limited space, we consider the chance small that a large distribution center will be established in our city.

118900.explanation: CDA Maassluis thinks it is important that Maassluis remains a hospitable and caring city, also for those who are fleeing. The current shelter in terms of number and approach suits the city that the municipality of Maassluis is.

118903.explanation: CDA Maassluis is not in favor of banning open fireplaces and wood stoves. CDA wants residents to think about the consequences of burning wood for themselves and their environment. CDA believes the municipality should actively communicate how to deal with wood burning and point out the health effects.

118908.explanation: CDA Maassluis values an inclusive society, which includes accessible buildings. We advise entrepreneurs on this, facilitate the Interest Group Inclusive Maassluis to provide advice. However, the entrepreneur must carry out the adjustments if desired.

118909.explanation: If this is a wish, it must proceed via the formal route of permit applications.

118910.explanation: We find it important that children and young adults growing up in poverty have opportunities to take swimming lessons, sports, or cultural activities such as music and dance. Adults can use the regular minimum income schemes.

118911.explanation: We consider the local broadcaster important for local news. The current budget is sufficient for this. Additionally, a national investigation is underway into whether local broadcasters should receive funding from the national government instead of the municipality.

118913.explanation: CDA Maassluis thinks it is important that all children get in touch with music lessons. This is subsidized and organized through the schools. For children growing up in poverty, there is the youth fund for sports and culture that can contribute.

118918.explanation: We find it important that everyone feels safe. Fixed and mobile cameras can help with this. If we deploy cameras, we naturally comply with privacy laws.

118919.explanation: We find it important that if someone is no longer safe at home, they can go to a safe place. Moreover, we also find it important that provisions are created aimed at preventing unsafe situations.

118925.explanation: We stimulate that people receiving social assistance continue to participate in society. We believe that very careful consideration must be given to the possibilities of the welfare recipient and that the human dimension should be applied.

118926.explanation: We think that the current debt forgiveness schemes should be maintained, and we maintain the healthcare cost allowance and the collective supplementary insurance for residents with an income up to 130 percent of the social assistance level.

118927.explanation: CDA Maassluis has committed itself in recent years to good poverty policy; the current income limits suffice.

118929.explanation: The current approach is sufficient and offers residents adequate public opportunities to charge.

118930.explanation: CDA Maassluis wants all streets to become 30 km zones, except for the east-west connection via Laan 1940-1945, Industrieweg, Mozartlaan, Westlandseweg, Kwartellaan, and Dr. A. Schweitzerdreef. Dr. Jan Schoutenlaan and PC Hooftlaan will therefore go from 50 km to a 30 km zone.

118931.explanation: The CDA Maassluis considers having choices in healthcare important. However, the municipality has hardly any means to influence this. For the care purchased by the municipality, the primary requirement is that it meets quality standards at realistic rates.

118936.explanation: There is currently no room for expansion.

118938.explanation: All municipalities have a national government mandate to provide housing for status holders in their city. We take into account in advance the number of status holders needing housing. Municipalities that fail to do so must be held accountable.

118940.explanation: CDA Maassluis supports solar energy as a sustainable energy source. However, we still see enough other places where solar panels can be installed.

118941.explanation: The swimming ability of our children is important. We are open to initiatives for school swimming.

118945.explanation: CDA Maassluis supports the current policy that allows limited room rental. This makes it possible to have rooms available for young people and students.

118946.explanation: Across the city there are already several 'informal' neighborhood centers where people can meet. We welcome more meeting places in new neighborhoods, such as Wilgenrijk and the Kade/van Gelder area. We want to better map out the needs of young people.

118948.explanation: CDA Maassluis acknowledges that in some neighborhoods it is somewhat harder to find a parking spot. Adding extra spaces often comes at the expense of something else, such as greenery. We take seriously residents’ ideas about how parking or streets could be arranged more conveniently.

118950.explanation: CDA Maassluis finds it important that Maassluis remains a hospitable and caring city, including for those who are fleeing. The current shelter capacity in terms of number and nature fits the city that Maassluis is.

118951.explanation: In the past period, we have started addressing bottlenecks such as Havenplein, Laan 1940-1945, and the Mozartlaan/Westlandseweg roundabout. We will continue this approach.

118894.explanation: We consider participation of utmost importance, but democratically elected representatives can and must weigh different interests and base their decisions on that.

118895.explanation: In Maassluis’s interest, we collaborate with residents, entrepreneurs, and associations. Where possible, this can also result in maintaining a park or managing a sports field, for example in public spaces.

118897.explanation: We are open to entrepreneurship. When it fits with the farming business and adds value for the city and surroundings, we view it positively.

118898.explanation: We find it important that when residents take initiative themselves, the municipality facilitates this and informs them about available (national) subsidies. After all, the benefits are also for the initiators.

118899.explanation: Within our densely built city, we have little space for this. If possibilities exist on industrial sites, we are positive about that.

118900.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the current shelter on asylum boats is temporary. The port should soon be available again for economic and historic activities.

118903.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves should be able to be used. However, we ask residents to use the burning guide to prevent nuisance.

118908.explanation: Accessibility is important; everyone should be able to participate. It is up to the entrepreneur how they run their business. No rules or obligations should be imposed on entrepreneurs.

118909.explanation: The scarce green space (Aalkeetpolder) we have should remain green. On the building area of farms, a 'kangoeroewoning' (granny flat) may be allowed with a permit.

118910.explanation: Sports are important for everyone. That is why we have the sports and culture fund. Membership should not be automatically paid for; there should be something in return, based on the principle of reciprocity.

118911.explanation: We support strong local media that inform residents well about what is happening in their area.

118913.explanation: Music lessons have a positive effect on the development of our children. Therefore, we focus on introductory lessons at a very low rate. Families with low incomes can receive subsidies for lessons through the youth sports and culture fund.

118918.explanation: The use of fixed cameras is important to make Maassluis safer. Not only at stations, but also at shopping centers, waste disposal points, and parking lots. Cameras increase the chance of catching offenders and have a preventive effect, contributing to a safer Maassluis for everyone.

118919.explanation: A women’s shelter is not necessarily required to be located in our city. This is a task that we take up at the regional level.

118925.explanation: If things go wrong temporarily, you receive benefits, but we expect an active attitude to return to work or otherwise contribute to the city.

118926.explanation: Long-term debt forgiveness is not a structural solution and does not fit an effective poverty policy. Policy must break poverty and strengthen financial self-reliance. Forgiveness remains limited to a small, demonstrably vulnerable group and not in cases of conscious or strategic behavior.

118927.explanation: Raising the income limit is not the solution. We must ensure that working pays off!

118929.explanation: We encourage electric driving, but the installation of charging points should, in our opinion, be based on data-driven information or at the request of multiple residents.

118930.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount! Therefore, we implement 30 km/h zones where needed, for example in neighborhoods, near schools, and sports complexes. The main roads through Maassluis remain 50 km/h.

118931.explanation: Everyone should have the freedom to choose and live their life according to their own beliefs. Therefore, people must also be free to choose the healthcare that suits them, within regular and existing budgets.

118936.explanation: Business activity has a positive influence on our city’s economy. It contributes to employment, so when opportunities arise, we are positive about them.

118938.explanation: We believe everyone should have equal opportunities in the allocation of social rental homes. No exceptions should be made for refugees with a residence permit.

118940.explanation: Maassluis has only a small percentage of agricultural land. If the owner wants to reduce or change activities, we view the installation of solar panels positively.

118941.explanation: We do not support providing money for school swimming. For education, this is a heavy burden and therefore not feasible. At the same time, we value good swimming education and find it important that all children obtain a diploma before their tenth birthday.

118945.explanation: We support sharing and splitting homes. We want to use available housing efficiently, and people should have the freedom to decide with whom they live, even if they are not a family. This is only intended for our residents and not for labor migrants.

118946.explanation: We see neighborhood centers and local meeting places as an asset to the neighborhood. We support facilities close to home, but this can also be realized differently through multifunctional use of buildings.

118948.explanation: We ensure sufficient parking spaces that fit the neighborhood, together with residents. Parking standards must be realistic: less car use does not mean people give up their cars. We seek creative parking solutions so everyone can park near their home.

118950.explanation: The shelter capacity for asylum seekers is fully utilized; there is no room for expanding shelter locations in Maassluis.

118951.explanation: With new housing and area development, accessibility is of utmost importance. Therefore, the connection of neighborhoods is a firm precondition for us.

118894.explanation: For D66, participation means the municipal council sometimes takes a step back. A referendum is a good way to present a current issue to all residents.

118895.explanation: People who are directly involved with their surroundings can often manage things better than the municipality. In Maassluis there are good examples of residents taking over tasks. D66 wants to promote that.

118897.explanation: Maassluis has few farms. It helps to experience nature and rural life by being able to buy local products at a farm or to camp there.

118898.explanation: D66 wants to support good initiatives that make our city more sustainable.

118899.explanation: Space in Maassluis is scarce. That is why D66 chooses innovative or sustainable companies. Distribution centers take up a lot of space, which comes at the expense of a greater number of small businesses.

118900.explanation: D66 wants Maassluis to take its role in relieving asylum seeker reception in the Netherlands. The reception on two asylum boats in the city center is going well.

118903.explanation: D66 wants a ban on burning on days when air quality and wind conditions are poor. Residents with health complaints experience nuisance from wood burning and as a municipality we must protect them.

118908.explanation: D66 wants the city to have no barriers to participation in everything Maassluis has to offer.

118909.explanation: D66 wants to keep the open landscape around Maassluis open and green. Housing construction does not fit there.

118910.explanation: D66 wants to remove barriers so children can develop themselves. Sports are part of that.

118911.explanation: Maassluis is fortunate to have good local and regional broadcasters. The WOS will become the regional broadcaster for our city. Good journalism is important for our democracy. D66 is willing to pay extra for that.

118913.explanation: Every child must have the opportunity to develop a hobby or passion.

118918.explanation: D66 closely monitors the protection of passers-by privacy. Fixed camera surveillance should only be considered as a last resort. The use of cameras is subject to strict rules. D66 is pleased with that.

118919.explanation: Maassluis is a small municipality. In some areas, we can better cooperate with neighboring municipalities such as Vlaardingen, Westland or Midden-Delfland. A women's shelter is important and can be arranged together with other municipalities.

118925.explanation: D66 chooses customized guidance towards employment instead of a requirement for all social assistance recipients.

118926.explanation: D66 supports a social municipality that is ready for the people who financially need help. Moreover, the municipality helps people with debts, so a payment arrears could become a problem for the municipality itself.

118927.explanation: Maassluis has good social policies that stimulate people to keep participating. Therefore, aid with poverty does not need to be made more widely available.

118929.explanation: Electric cars emit fewer harmful substances than petrol cars. D66 wants the municipality to encourage the use of electric cars.

118930.explanation: D66 chooses for the greatest traffic safety. The chance of injury for pedestrians or cyclists is much higher at 50 km/h than at 30 km/h. D66 wants 30 km/h to become the standard in Maassluis.

118931.explanation: D66 supports inclusive care that takes all cultural and personal differences into account.

118936.explanation: Space in Maassluis is scarce. D66 chooses companies with a lot of knowledge or employees on few square meters.

118938.explanation: This is already the case because the municipality has the legal task to provide housing for status holders. A home is a prerequisite for participation in the city and D66 finds it important that all residents can participate.

118940.explanation: There is little agricultural land available in Maassluis, but D66 finds locations where collective solar energy can be generated very useful.

118941.explanation: In Maassluis we have abolished school swimming. That was not a financial choice, but the schools preferred it themselves. D66 sees no reason to reintroduce school swimming in Maassluis.

118945.explanation: D66 wants to allow this under conditions. By splitting up houses, we want to make more homes available in the short term. However, these smaller homes must be of good quality.

118948.explanation: D66 wants residential neighborhoods to become places where it is pleasant to stay, play or walk. Parking spaces come at the expense of the scarce space or green areas. Therefore, we do not want to create more parking spaces in existing neighborhoods.

118950.explanation: D66 wants Maassluis to meet its obligation for the reception of asylum seekers and status holders. Reception boats are not ideal, we prefer decent housing.

118951.explanation: New residential neighborhoods in Maassluis are already well accessible by car. D66 chooses good connections to public transport, especially since the neighborhoods are close to metro stations.

parties.name: Partij van de Arbeid (PvdA) / GroenLinks Maassluis
parties.fullName: Partij van de Arbeid (PvdA) / GroenLinks Maassluis

118894.explanation: Residents should be able to give their opinion much more often through substantive participation processes. Referenda simplify complicated issues: the council is elected by the residents and appointed to make the trade-offs weighing all aspects.

118895.explanation: In Maassluis we have been working with district deals for years, where involved residents take over parts of the management of their own neighborhood. That works well; involvement in the neighborhood and with neighbors then increases!

118897.explanation: It is good if farmers find new uses for their farm that reduce nitrogen emissions. But the 'always allow' approach is a risk for the cluttering of the rural area. Our Aalkeetpolder must remain green.

118898.explanation: Generating sustainable energy together is important for a clean future and social cohesion. We want to support initiatives from residents, in words and where necessary also with money.

118899.explanation: Space for companies on our industrial sites in Maassluis is limited, we want as much employment as possible there and therefore no large distribution center with few jobs.

118900.explanation: Every municipality in the Netherlands must provide part of the asylum reception; Maassluis does that properly and without nuisance. Closing our asylum reception would be nice but can only happen when the reception elsewhere in the Netherlands is no longer overcrowded.

118903.explanation: Wood burning causes fine particles and can lead to nuisance and health risks for people with lung problems when there is little wind. We want to counter wood burning at those times. But when it is windy enough, it can happen without nuisance, so a ban goes too far.

118908.explanation: It is important that everyone can enter the place where they need to be. That is inclusion: thinking of residents who have poor sight, are mobility impaired, etc. It is up to entrepreneurs themselves to comply with the guidelines for that; municipality funds are for municipal buildings.

118909.explanation: The Aalkeetpolder must remain open. Therefore, we cannot allow houses to be built on the few remaining pieces of meadow.

118910.explanation: Life on social assistance is not a picnic, but it is also not impossible to pay sports membership fees. Giving everyone on social assistance that is too generous and expensive. For children growing up in poverty (including those from working parents), there is a scheme that allows them to play sports. That must be maintained.

118911.explanation: The local broadcaster is very important for reliable news. The WOS already receives - in accordance with the legal obligation - a substantial amount from the municipality of Maassluis.

118913.explanation: Every child should be introduced to culture and music at primary school - at no cost. Children who want to continue can learn further through the subsidized music school. Music lessons only need to be reimbursed for children whose parents cannot afford them.

118918.explanation: Cameras alone do not provide safety, but together with good lighting, involved residents who report quickly, and municipal enforcement officers, cameras work well. They prevent crime and help with investigation if something does happen. We are in favor of cameras in shopping centers and near metro stations.

118919.explanation: Domestic violence unfortunately occurs far too often. Sometimes abused people return to a violent partner because there is no other place to stay. That is why women's shelters are very important, and Maassluis has a role in this and is already involved.

118925.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks Maassluis wants to get as many people as possible into work. Therefore, residents receiving benefits should be helped to find work. If that is no longer possible, volunteer work can be a good workplace. This must be tailored individually, because it is not possible everywhere and does not suit everyone.

118926.explanation: Residents with the lowest incomes especially struggle with high prices. For them, the removal of debt forgiveness (only waste disposal charges are forgiven) would mean a cost increase of 405 euros (single person) or 477 euros (family); equivalent to a month’s groceries.

118927.explanation: It is good not to stop poverty schemes immediately at 100, 110, or 120% of the social minimum. Then residents who start earning a bit more regress. As with the Rotterdam Pass, a half-price rate for residents between 120% and 150% of the social minimum prevents negative effects.

118929.explanation: The number of parking spaces for electric cars must follow the need for charging points: simply adding more charging points leads to empty, cordoned-off parking spaces and fewer spots for regular cars. Too few places are bad for the transition to electric driving.

118930.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks Maassluis stands for road safety and reducing noise and nuisance from speeding. Almost all of Maassluis already has a 30 km/h limit except for a few main roads: these must remain 50 km/h for emergency services and buses.

118931.explanation: It is great when residents and care providers are a good match. Therefore, a diverse and culturally sensitive care offer is valuable. But meeting care needs is more important than a 'match', and through neutral care providers everyone can be helped, which is already difficult in this labor market.

118936.explanation: There is no more room for expansion of industrial sites. New business activity is still possible at the Lely campus and when companies close or relocate in existing business buildings. Also with the renewal of Kapelpolder.

118938.explanation: Maassluis must fulfill the legal assignment to house status holders who need to be accommodated. It helps if this is low cost and spread throughout the city. This is currently done through direct mediation, just as with people after a care trajectory or imprisonment.

118940.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks Maassluis supports the sustainability of the energy supply. Provided that it fits well into the landscape (for example with hedges around it) and does no damage to nature in the polder, this is possible.

118941.explanation: In a country and city full of water, being able to swim can sometimes be a matter of life and death. Therefore, PvdA-GroenLinks wants to reinstate school swimming. Also, for children who already have a swimming diploma, it is good to keep up swimming skills in the form of aquatic gymnastics.

118945.explanation: We want to combat exploitation and overcrowding and have strict rules in Maassluis. At the same time, in times of housing shortage, we also want to make smarter use of existing homes in the coming years: especially in large houses, shared housing is part of the solution.

118946.explanation: We want to strengthen social cohesion, meeting, and inclusion. In every neighborhood there are opportunities to enhance social meetings, this can also be at existing institutions and organizations or schools. We can open them more often to the neighborhood.

118948.explanation: Parking in existing neighborhoods is often a bottleneck; in good consultation with residents, neighborhood- and/or street-specific solutions can be found, provided that sufficient space for greenery remains.

118950.explanation: In Maassluis we almost fully comply with the assignment of former Minister Faber: we accommodate 140 asylum seekers on two houseboats, house Ukrainians in flexible housing, meet the quota for status holders, and work on integration.

118951.explanation: In new neighborhoods such as Station West and the Van Gelder site, there will be smaller, affordable homes for starters and seniors, so there will be fewer cars. But these new neighborhoods must also be well accessible by car: therefore we invest in infrastructure and traffic flow, including on Laan 1940-1945.

118894.explanation: We see that referendums are no solution for complex political issues. Moreover, a consultative referendum's outcome is not binding. This causes confusion and dissatisfaction. A (broad) survey is a better alternative when the municipality wants to gauge residents' opinions.

118895.explanation: If residents can perform tasks better, smarter, or more humanely, the municipality should allow space and show trust.

118897.explanation: Here applies 'yes, unless.' It should be possible unless there are other overriding interests.

118898.explanation: Local energy cooperatives give residents control over their own energy. We want to actively support them with knowledge, manpower, and guarantees so that sustainable energy truly becomes by and for Maassluis.

118899.explanation: Maassluis is economically lagging. We are open to new initiatives and employment, as long as they fit the scale of the city and contribute to a healthy, livable environment.

118900.explanation: We hope for peace in the world so that asylum accommodation is no longer needed! Until then, we help a fellow human in need. The distribution law is a good way to fairly allocate the task throughout the Netherlands. The national government must improve overall asylum policy.

118903.explanation: We apply a ban on burning in unfavorable weather conditions. This is to improve air quality and protect residents' health.

118908.explanation: Accessibility requirements are laid down in legislation. It is up to every entrepreneur to ensure their business complies with the law and is attractive to city residents with disabilities.

118909.explanation: In Maassluis it is important that the transition from the built-up city to the green remains recognizable. Fragmentation and hardening at the edges of Maassluis must be prevented.

118910.explanation: Every child must be able to participate. We invest extra in sports and cultural participation for children from low-income families.

118911.explanation: Independent local and regional media are crucial for local news provision and local democracy. They inform residents, keep administrators sharp, and set societal issues on the agenda. Sufficient financial resources must be available for these purposes.

118913.explanation: Music and cultural programs are important for all primary school pupils. Additionally, we make extra funds available for sports and cultural participation for children from low-income families.

118918.explanation: Camera surveillance is a good tool, but privacy legislation must be carefully observed. When needed, an additional camera can be purchased. The placement of cameras by residents has a good preventive effect. Of course, rules also apply here.

118919.explanation: There is currently an investigation into sufficient shelter facilities. We are collaborating with Vlaardingen and Schiedam on this.

118925.explanation: Everyone receiving social assistance participates in society where possible. We find it important that people are encouraged to stay active and develop talents. For example, through volunteer work, a learning trajectory, or another form of social contribution.

118926.explanation: For those who cannot manage on their own, we take care together, and the government acts as a shield for the vulnerable. Maassluis applies a social debt forgiveness and collection policy when collecting municipal taxes and levies to prevent problematic debts.

118927.explanation: Maassluis implements an active poverty policy. Assistance is offered in various ways. Think also of energy measures and support for children. Not all existing possibilities are always used yet.

118929.explanation: A recent improvement has been made in this area.

118930.explanation: 30 km/h makes the city safer, cleaner, and more livable. We favor this standard, with tailored measures per street.

118931.explanation: For effective care, it is important that the care fits who you are.

118936.explanation: Maassluis is economically lagging. There is not much space left in Maassluis. Where opportunities arise, it is important to strengthen the business community.

118938.explanation: That is already current policy. Choosing interim solutions does not solve the housing shortage. The group of status holders is small but does require attention in housing.

118940.explanation: Here too applies 'yes, unless.' In principle, it is allowed as long as the consequences for the landscape remain within limits.

118941.explanation: Every child in Maassluis is proficient in swimming by the end of primary school age.

118945.explanation: Renting out rooms in a boarding house can also help combat the housing shortage.

118946.explanation: The neighborhood is a priority in the coming years. We support community centers where people come together to be heard.

118948.explanation: We encourage less car use, but sufficient parking spaces remain necessary. These may be located at the edges of the neighborhood.

118950.explanation: Maassluis contributes its share, but other municipalities must do so as well. The burdens must be distributed fairly.

118951.explanation: The current construction plans are too ambitious and are causing the city to become gridlocked. We want plans that better fit our city: tranquility, realism, and accessible neighborhoods. Additionally, the bicycle is a suitable alternative mode of transport.

118894.explanation: Democracy should be more than just voting every four years. Residents should be able to express their opinions on important issues in the interim. This also keeps politicians accountable throughout the four years.

118895.explanation: The management of public facilities is a core task of the government, and we do not support shifting that responsibility onto residents.

118897.explanation: Farmers manage their own land and operations, and we support entrepreneurial freedom: extra activities help farmers remain financially healthy without subsidies. Of course, these activities must take the surroundings into account.

118898.explanation: Residents who want to collectively generate sustainable energy should be able to do so without government support.

118899.explanation: Distribution centers cause extra traffic, pollution, and few permanent jobs; we choose livability and space for local entrepreneurs.

118900.explanation: Our culture, housing market, and welfare state cannot handle the ongoing mass immigration. We refuse to facilitate the current mismanagement and want a local asylum stop. Therefore, the asylum boats must leave. We stand for regional reception. That is why we take in Ukrainians (limited).

118903.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves are cozy, and we love coziness. At a time when more and more bans are imposed, the question arises: are we going to take away this small piece of people's joy in life as well? No.

118908.explanation: Municipal financial support is fine, provided the entrepreneur also invests themselves. Accessibility is primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur.

118909.explanation: Farmers manage their own land. Adding a few houses here and there is no problem, but allowing housing construction on agricultural land can lead to landscape disorder, set a precedent, and increase value pressure on surrounding lands.

118910.explanation: People can make use of the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture. Additional financial support increases dependence on government aid and raises the burden on taxpayers. Social assistance is intended as a safety net for necessary living expenses, not for supplementary provisions.

118911.explanation: The municipality already provides sufficient financial support to the regional broadcaster WOS. Additional financial support leads to unnecessarily higher public expenditure.

118913.explanation: Basic skills such as reading, writing, and arithmetic are declining and therefore have priority. Municipal subsidies for music lessons lead to extra expenses and shift focus away from essential educational goals. Music education is a non-essential addition.

118918.explanation: Camera surveillance enhances safety and enforcement. It discourages crime, facilitates the detection of offenders, and gives residents a sense of security. Leefbaar Maessluys is a party of Order & Justice, and safety is our top priority.

118919.explanation: We protect vulnerable citizens, such as victims of domestic violence. A local shelter provides safety, prevents escalation of violence, and supports families in need. Domestic violence is unacceptable, and the municipality must take concrete action to protect victims.

118925.explanation: Personal responsibility, self-reliance, and social involvement are central to us. This prevents dependence on the government, contributes to the local community, and ensures that tax money is used productively.

118926.explanation: Taxes are intended to collectively finance the municipality’s core tasks, not as long-term subsidies for individuals. Ending debt forgiveness promotes self-reliance, ensures a fairer distribution of burdens, and limits unnecessary taxpayer expenses.

118927.explanation: Raising the income threshold increases dependence on the government, leads to higher expenses, and undermines both incentives for personal responsibility and the municipality’s financial capacity.

118929.explanation: There must be a sufficient and proportional number of charging stations for electric cars that matches demand. This does not mean we should increase the number of charging stations in advance.

118930.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h is unnecessary and inefficient. It impedes traffic flow and increases travel times. Tailoring measures per street or neighborhood is wiser than a general rule. On main access roads, 50 km/h should remain the standard.

118931.explanation: Care provision should be based on professional standards and equal treatment, not on identity criteria that unnecessarily pressure the efficiency and coherence of the care system and promote segregation. In Maassluis, Dutch culture is leading.

118936.explanation: We must use the scarce space in Maassluis carefully. First, existing business parks must be better utilized. Unrestrained expansion can lead to landscape degradation, extra traffic pressure, and loss of livability.

118938.explanation: Since July 1, 2017, municipalities are no longer required to do so. This priority is unfair to our own residents who have often been on a waiting list for years. Social rental homes must be allocated based on equal rules and waiting time. Newcomers must therefore wait their turn in line.

118940.explanation: Agricultural land should primarily be used for food production and landscape preservation. Installing solar panels on agricultural land reduces biodiversity and damages the landscape. Sustainable energy should be generated on roofs, fallow land, or industrial sites.

118941.explanation: Mastering basic skills-such as swimming-prevents accidents, contributes to children’s health and self-reliance, and increases their safety. This is especially important in the water-rich environment of Maassluis.

118945.explanation: Renting out individual rooms can quickly lead to overcrowding and nuisance. People also have the right to a decent home.

118946.explanation: We support community centers, taking into account demand and capacity per neighborhood.

118948.explanation: Neighborhoods must be easily accessible for residents and visitors without parking causing nuisance or frustration. Sufficient parking spaces support local entrepreneurs, families, and the elderly.

118950.explanation: Our culture, housing market, and welfare state cannot handle the ongoing mass immigration. We refuse to facilitate the current mismanagement and want a local asylum stop. Therefore, the asylum boats must leave. We stand for regional reception, and we take in Ukrainians (limited).

118951.explanation: Residents must be able to use their car efficiently for shopping, work, and other daily activities. Better accessibility prevents traffic and parking pressure in existing neighborhoods.

statements.title: New residential districts should be made more accessible by car.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The residents of Maassluis should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Maassluis moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Maassluis should continue to allow the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves.

titleParts.text: In Maassluis moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Maassluis should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Business parks in Maassluis should be allowed to expand.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Maassluis should increase CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should do more for the reception of asylum seekers and people with residence permits.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer doen voor de opvang van

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
